segments.text: PART 1
CAROL: It's really good to see you, Tom.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：见到你真的很高兴，汤姆。

segments.text: Since I had to give up work, I feel I'm losing touch with my friends and colleagues.
segments.translation: 自从我不得不放弃工作以来，我感觉自己正在与朋友和同事失去联系。

segments.text: TOM: We really miss you in the office – we were all so sorry you had to leave.
segments.translation: 汤姆：我们在办公室真的很想念你 —— 你不得不离开，我们都很难过。

segments.text: But you must be relieved to have more time to look after your mother. How is she?
segments.translation: 但你一定松了一口气，因为有更多时间照顾你的母亲了。她怎么样？

segments.text: CAROL: Well, she's very cheerful but she needs a lot of help.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：嗯，她很开朗，但她需要很多帮助。

segments.text: TOM: Have you tried to get any support from the local council?
segments.translation: 汤姆：你有没有试图从当地议会获得任何支持？

segments.text: CAROL: No. I didn't know I could.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：没有。我不知道我能做到。

segments.text: TOM: Yes, they offer different kinds of practical support.
segments.translation: 汤姆：是的，他们提供了各种实际支持。

segments.text: They realise that carers sometimes need time for all the other responsibilities they have apart from the person they're caring for, and also that they sometimes need a break.
segments.translation: 他们意识到，照顾者有时需要时间来承担除照顾对象之外的其他所有责任，而且他们有时也需要休息。

segments.text: CAROL: Absolutely. OK, so tell me more. How do I go about getting this support?
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：当然。好吧，那么再多告诉我一些。我该如何获得这种支持呢？

segments.text: TOM: Well, you'd have to have an assessment of your mother's needs.
segments.translation: 汤姆：嗯，你需要对你母亲的需求进行评估。

segments.text: That means someone would come round and talk to you about the situation and what you need.
segments.translation: 这意味着会有人来跟你谈谈情况和你的需求。

segments.text: So for a start they'd want to know the amount of time you spend looking after your mother every day.
segments.translation: 首先，他们会想知道你每天花多少时间照顾你的母亲。

segments.text: CAROL: OK.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：好的。

segments.text: TOM: Then they'll probably ask you what sorts of tasks you do for your mother during the day.
segments.translation: 汤姆：那么他们可能会问你白天都为母亲做了哪些事情。

segments.text: Things like if she needs help with getting dressed, for example.
segments.translation: 比如说，如果她需要帮忙穿衣服。

segments.text: CAROL: Right. I help her with that, and also I help her get into the shower in the morning.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：好的，我会帮她做这些，我还会帮她早上洗澡。

segments.text: TOM: Yes, that sort of thing.
segments.translation: 汤姆：是的，就是那种事。

segments.text: They'll probably ask you if you do the shopping for her, and help her at mealtimes.
segments.translation: 他们可能会问你是否愿意帮她购物，以及在用餐时间帮忙。

segments.text: And whether she can cope using money.
segments.translation: 还有她是否能应付使用金钱。

segments.text: CAROL: Yes, that's becoming a bit of a problem.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：是的，这确实变成了一个小问题。

segments.text: She used to be very good at it, but not any more.
segments.translation: 她以前很擅长这个，但现在不行了。

segments.text: TOM: And be ready to tell them about anything you find particularly difficult about caring for your mother.
segments.translation: 汤姆：准备好告诉他们任何你觉得特别困难的关于照顾你母亲的事情。

segments.text: CAROL: So recently I've noticed she's started to have quite bad problems with her memory … if I wasn't there, I think she'd forget to eat, for example.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：最近我注意到她的记忆力开始出现相当严重的问题...... 比如，如果我不在场，我想她可能会忘记吃饭。

segments.text: And often she doesn't seem quite sure what day it is.
segments.translation: 而且她经常似乎不太确定今天是星期几。

segments.text: TOM: Yes, tell them about that. And are there any physical difficulties you have caring for her? Lifting her, for example?
segments.translation: 汤姆：是的，告诉他们这件事。你照顾她时有什么身体上的困难吗？比如说，举起她？

segments.text: CAROL: Yes. She's quite heavy, and I'm afraid of hurting my back – I'd be in real trouble if that happened.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：是的。她很重，我担心会伤到背 —— 如果真的发生那种情况，我会遇到真正的麻烦。

segments.text: TOM: They can give you advice about that, and also about how to avoid the possibility of your mum having a fall.
segments.translation: 汤姆：他们可以给你一些建议，比如如何避免你妈妈摔倒的可能性。

segments.text: CAROL: Great. So once they've done this assessment, if I'm eligible, what happens next?
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：太好了。那么，一旦他们完成了这个评估，如果我符合条件，接下来会发生什么呢？

segments.text: TOM: Well, they might support you financially.
segments.translation: 汤姆：嗯，他们可能会在经济上支持你。

segments.text: So they might help you with transport costs, like if you have to get a taxi to take your mother for an appointment, for example.
segments.translation: 所以他们可能会帮你支付交通费用，比如说，如果你需要叫出租车送你母亲去赴约。

segments.text: CAROL: I usually drive her myself actually. So could I claim for the petrol?
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：实际上我通常自己开车送她。那么我能申请汽油费吗？

segments.text: TOM: You could, and you can claim for the insurance too.
segments.translation: 汤姆：你可以，你也可以申请保险。

segments.text: CAROL: Oh, right.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：哦，好的。

segments.text: TOM: And if you need help with the housework, they can arrange for someone to come along once or twice a week.
segments.translation: 汤姆：如果你需要帮忙做家务，他们可以安排人每周来一两次。

segments.text: And one other thing, I hope you don't mind me saying this, but it's important you look after yourself, and it seems to me you're under quite a bit of stress.
segments.translation: 还有一件事，我希望你不介意我这么说，但你要好好照顾自己，这很重要。在我看来，你似乎压力很大。

segments.text: CAROL: I am, yes.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：是的。

segments.text: TOM: Well, tell the council, because they may be able to give you some advice on how to minimise it.
segments.translation: 汤姆：好吧，告诉委员会，因为他们可能会给你一些如何最小化压力的建议。

segments.text: CAROL: Really? Though actually I feel so much better having talked to you.
segments.translation: 卡萝尔：真的吗？不过说实话，和你谈过之后我感觉好多了。

segments.text: I'll get in touch with the council straight away. Now shall we …
segments.translation: 我马上就联系委员会。现在我们是不是......
